so that we do not need to ﬁnd a numerical solution of the dif-
fusion equation in ﬁtting the model to the measured results.

THICKNESS PROFILE AND THE PENETRATION
DEPTH
In an ALD growth experiment, N cycles of reactant
pulses consisting of the ﬁrst reactant (e.g., TMA) and the
second reactant (H
2O) are fed into the channel, and the
resulting thickness proﬁles(x) is measured.

When the pene-
tration depth xp is determined by measuring the ﬁlm thick-
ness s, the error in it is
Dxp ¼ Ds
jds=dxj; (33)
where Ds is the error in ﬁlm thickness andds/dx is the slope
of the ﬁlm thickness vs.

The maximum value of the slope is at the point of half
ﬁlm thickness and determiningx
p at this point gives the best
accuracy.

Therefore, we deﬁne the half-thickness penetration
xp of the ﬁlm growth, so that the ﬁlm thickness is 50% of
thickness s(0) at the beginning of the channel, or by solving
xp in the equation
sx pðÞ ¼ s 0ðÞ
2 : (34)
The ﬁlm grows on both the top and bottom surfaces of the
channel.

COMPARISON WITH THE GORDON MODEL
In the Gordon model for ALD conformality, it is
assumed that the gas enters the rectangular channel (height
H /C28 width W) as a front with a constant concentration with-
out any gas diffusion in the propagation direction (x-axis).
